news.headline: Paradigm Capital names Perimeter and maintains Profound as healthcare Top Picks
news.summary: Paradigm Capital initiated Perimeter as a healthcare Top Pick with a Speculative Buy and a C$0.95 price target (stock at C$0.275 on July 3). The analyst frames the March FDA approval of Claire as the accelerant for U.S. commercialization and flags M&A optionality, noting the stock trades at ~2.3x 2027e revenue versus ~10.3x average for comparable MedTech takeouts.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI to Participate in the Planet MicroCap Showcase in Las Vegas
news.summary: CEO Adrian Mendes presented Perimeter's post-FDA commercialization strategy and upcoming milestones at the Planet MicroCap Showcase on June 17. The appearance underscores the push for broader small-cap investor visibility following Claire's approval.

news.headline: Perimeter Selected for INOVAIT Pilot Fund to Advance AI for Breast Cancer Surgery
news.summary: Perimeter secured up to ~C$148,000 from Canada's INOVAIT Pilot Fund plus C$100,000 in Mitacs funding to develop the next AI generation for Claire alongside the University of Toronto. The amount is small, but it is non-dilutive external validation of the AI platform.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI Closes Final Tranche of Non-Brokered Private Placement
news.summary: Perimeter closed the final tranche of its convertible-debenture placement, bringing the total to C$6.04M. Insider alignment is strong: CEO Mendes subscribed C$2.76M and Chamath Palihapitiya's SC Master Holdings (Social Capital) C$2.78M, though the notes add potential dilution.

news.headline: Perimeter Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Q1 recurring revenue rose ~14% YoY to roughly $385k, operating expenses fell ~30% to $3.2M and the net loss narrowed to ~$3.0M. Cash at end of March was only ~$421k (before the ~$7.5M LIFE offering in May); Perimeter reported its first two commercial Claire placements and a funnel of ~50 qualified leads.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI Closes LIFE Offering (~$7.5M)
news.summary: Perimeter closed its LIFE offering for roughly $7.5M in gross proceeds, shoring up a nearly empty balance sheet and funding the U.S. launch of Claire. Combined with the convertible notes, the near-term liquidity crunch is addressed at the cost of further dilution.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report (expected August) covering the first full quarter of Claire commercialization; watch new placements from the ~50-lead funnel and cash burn versus the May/June financings.

catalysts.label: Claire rollout / new clinic deals
catalysts.detail: Additional commercial Claire placements at U.S. hospitals expected, with more than a dozen leads in active Prospecting/Developing/Validating/Contracting stages.

catalysts.label: Medicare reimbursement decision
catalysts.detail: Per Paradigm Capital, a reimbursement determination expected around year-end would be the most important driver of Claire adoption economics.

catalysts.label: Possible Nasdaq up-listing
catalysts.detail: Cited by Paradigm Capital as a potential catalyst to broaden U.S. investor access; no official timeline announced.

pulse: As of July 13, 2026, Perimeter remains in early Claire commercialization mode following March's FDA PMA, with no material company news since the July 6 sell-side coverage; the ~50-lead sales funnel and pace of new hospital placements remain the key things to watch into Q2 results (expected August). The May LIFE offering (~$7.5M) and June's C$6.04M convertible-note placement, heavily subscribed by CEO Mendes and Chamath Palihapitiya's Social Capital, stabilized a previously thin cash position, and Paradigm Capital's July 6 Top Pick call (Speculative Buy, C$0.95 target vs. ~C$0.275 spot) has kept sentiment constructive. The stock remains a speculative micro-cap awaiting evidence of real commercial traction, with a possible year-end Medicare reimbursement decision and a talked-about Nasdaq up-listing as the next major catalysts.
